title: clematis alpina var. macropetala
intro: Clematis alpina var. macropetala, also known as the Alpine Clematis or Large-flowered Alpenliebe, is a flowering plant species within the Clematis genus, characterized by its white and pink nodding flowers with 4-6 petal segments. Traditionally, the plant has been used in herbal medicine, particularly for its astringent, anti-inflammatory, and antiseptic properties, which are attributed to its flavonoid and alkaloid compounds. The health benefits of Clematis alpina var. macropetala include its potential to aid in the treatment of fever, infections, and skin conditions, as well as its ability to promote wound healing and reduce inflammation. In herbal preparations, the plant is typically used in infusions, decoctions, or tinctures, often combined with other herbs to enhance its therapeutic effects.
